contract.summary: Show the resolved repo-owned workspace config layered onto product defaults.

reads.surface: verbose flag for broad diagnostic output
field selector for exact JSON drill-down

steps.description: Load workspace config, defaults, local override state, and the full config payload.
Return only requested JSON fields when --select is provided.
Emit resolved config with contract-declared text views or JSON.

guards: Report config without writing repo state.
Treat missing repo config as product-default resolution.
Prefer --select for exact field drill-down and --verbose only for broad diagnostics.
proof: json output remains compatible with existing config command tests
config.report uses command-generation-owned payload.project and output.emit text_views for exact field projection

skill.instruction_summary: Use this operation when an agent needs the effective workspace config.

ir_plan.summary: Config report executes generated operation IR through codegen-owned primitive dataflow; payload.project owns exact selected-output projection and output.emit consumes contract-declared config text views.

steps.description: Resolve and validate the target root for config lookup.
Load workspace config, defaults, local overrides, valid module metadata, and full config payload.
Project requested fields and emit report output through a reusable operation-local fragment.

fragments.description: Reusable report tail that projects requested fields and emits the command output.

steps.description: Project requested exact fields from the config payload.
Emit config output through portable output.emit using declared config text views.

contract.summary: Append one local-only delegation outcome record for target-profile tuning.

steps.description: Append the local-only delegation outcome record.

guards: Required outcome fields must be present and enum-valid.
New admissions must include independent scope context.
Routable authority is derived from producer provenance; untrusted or agent self-observed signals are stored as non-routing uncertainty.
Lifecycle transitions must reference an existing predecessor id.
Append enforces bounded per-context retention before assignment consumes the ledger.
Do not treat local outcome records as checked-in repo policy.
proof: delegation outcome append tests remain compatible
delegation outcome text output stays in generated output rather than lifecycle text fallback

ir_plan.summary: Delegation outcome append executes generated operation IR through codegen-owned primitive dataflow while workspace runtime performs the local-only append compatibility operation and generated output owns the text result.

steps.description: Resolve and validate the target root for the local-only delegation outcome file.
Append the local-only delegation outcome record.
Emit the append result in the requested format.

schema.description: Input accepted by public assignment lifecycle operations that prepare handoff packets, import returns, admit or reject results, integrate admitted work, and record lifecycle maintenance actions.

admission_status.description: Reviewer decision recorded for an imported assignment return.

artifact_ref.description: Path or durable reference to a lifecycle artifact, such as an export packet or imported return record.

assignment_id.description: Stable Planning assignment identifier for the delegated run.

assignment_revision.description: Current assignment authority revision that the lifecycle operation is scoped to.

assignment_gate_json.description: Serialized current assignment gate resolved by the workspace before export/admission.

assignment_policy_json.description: Serialized current assignment policy resolved by the workspace before export/admission.

aw_proof_receipt_json.description: Serialized AW-owned proof receipt resolved by the workspace before export/admission.

current_authority_ref.description: Live authority reference resolved by the receiving workspace before admission or protected mutation.

delegation_decision_json.description: Serialized current delegation decision resolved by the workspace before export/admission.

dry_run.description: Whether to compute the lifecycle transition without applying local artifact writes.

expires_at.description: Optional expiration timestamp for temporary overrides or lifecycle decisions.

live_mutation_baseline.description: Current protected mutation baseline observed by the admitting workspace.

packet_json.description: Serialized assignment handoff packet for import or repair workflows.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for rejection, repair, reassignment, cleanup, close, or override actions.

return_id.description: Stable identifier for a returned assignment result packet.

return_json.description: Serialized worker return payload awaiting admission.

run_state_json.description: Serialized current run-state authority resolved by the workspace before export/admission.

run_id.description: Stable assignment run identifier used for local lifecycle artifact storage.

scope.description: Bounded scope for reassignment, repair, cleanup, close, or override records.

target.description: Target repository path for the lifecycle operation.

target_name.description: Named target or worker lane that receives the assignment packet.

transport.description: Transport channel used for the assignment packet or returned result.

schema.title: Assignment lifecycle operation input

schema.description: Result emitted by public assignment lifecycle operations, including transition status, mutation effect, local artifacts, and recovery guidance.

artifact_refs.description: Local artifact references written or inspected during the lifecycle transition.

kind.description: Versioned result envelope discriminator for assignment lifecycle operations.

mutation_applied.description: Whether the operation applied a local lifecycle state mutation.

operation_id.description: Public operation id that produced this lifecycle result.

outcome.description: High-level result category for automation and closeout proof.

reason_code.description: Machine-readable reason for blocked, failed, or no-op outcomes when available.

recovery_command.description: Suggested command or action to recover from a blocked lifecycle transition.

run_id.description: Assignment run identifier associated with the transition.

state.description: Current local lifecycle state after applying or evaluating the transition.

status.description: Specific lifecycle status, such as handoff-prepared, awaiting-admission, admitted, integrated, or blocked.

transition.description: Lifecycle transition name executed by the operation.

schema.title: Assignment lifecycle operation result

schema.description: External client input contract for the config.report operation.

verbose.description: Whether to request the full config projection.

schema.title: Correction event operation input
schema.description: Input accepted by generated correction-event lifecycle operations.

subjects_json.description: Optional serialized target identity subjects for fixtures; normal operation resolves config subjects.

trusted_authority_receipt_json.description: Trusted host/proof receipt that may admit human/review/evaluator authority.

idempotency_key.description: Optional stable key used to identify repeated deliveries.

delivery_id.description: Stable delivery identifier for this correction event.

target_identity_ref.description: Target identity, name, or alias to resolve before admission.

target_revision.description: Submitted target revision associated with the correction.

source_ref.description: Stable source reference for audit and idempotency.

producer_class.description: Producer class used for authority resolution.

producer_id.description: Producer identity used for audit and authority resolution.

authority.description: Claimed correction authority; not trusted without a trusted authority receipt.

desired_behavior.description: Behavior the correction says should replace the previous behavior.

replaced_behavior.description: Behavior boundary being corrected or superseded.

invariant_id.description: Stable semantic invariant identifier for deduplication.

behavior_class.description: Stable behavior class for semantic correction identity.

task_class.description: Task class where the correction applies.

scope_class.description: Scope class where the correction applies.

route_decisions.description: Requested routing decisions for trusted correction events.

evidence_hash.description: Hash of evidence supporting the correction.

evidence_ref.description: Reference to evidence supporting the correction.

predecessor_event_id.description: Prior correction event being disputed, withdrawn, or superseded.

dry_run.description: When true, report the intended operation without applying a storage mutation.

schema.title: Correction event operation result
schema.description: Result envelope emitted by generated correction-event lifecycle operations.

operation_id.description: Correction-event operation identifier that produced the result.

mutation_applied.description: Whether local correction-event storage was changed.

store_ref.description: Repository-relative correction-event store path, when available.

receipt_ref.description: Repository-relative operation receipt path, when available.

admission.description: Admission, rejection, low-authority, retention, and routing details.

schema.description: External client input contract for delegation-outcome.append.

predecessor_id.description: Existing record id for lifecycle transition operations.

authority.description: Requested authority class for the evidence. Runtime derives routability from producer provenance.

confidence.description: Confidence classification for the admitted evidence.

source_type.description: Source type for provenance, such as proof-receipt, review-comment, telemetry, or local-json-ledger.

source_ref.description: Exact source reference used for duplicate/idempotency and audit.

producer_class.description: Producer class resolved by the operation boundary.

assignment_route.description: Assignment route observed for this outcome.

proof_observation.description: Proof observation associated with the outcome.

review_observation.description: Review observation associated with the outcome.

uncertainty_state.description: Uncertainty state preserved for non-routing evidence.

idempotency_key.description: Stable idempotency key supplied by the producer.

schema.description: Structured failure envelope returned by an external operation invocation.

command.description: Command surface that failed when supplied.

exit_status.description: Positive process exit status when supplied.

compatibility_rule: Protocol major versions must match; fingerprint changes require requirement negotiation.
